scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Subtle Bug: Event Loop Blocking
justification: All pass criteria met: parseXmlSync identified as Critical event loop blocking issue; 200 webhook/minute + 2MB + sync = event loop saturation clearly explained with quantified impact (16.5% capacity loss, cascading failures); async parsing recommended with concrete code example (xml2js.parseStringPromise); early-response + failure inconsistency identified as Critical finding #1 with detailed explanation of silent data loss; idempotency concern explicitly noted as an Important issue; safe_to_deploy is false with clear reasoning.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the fire-and-forget issue — quantified data loss rate (2,880/day at 1% failure), provided both quick fix and production-grade queue-based solution
Event loop blocking analysis includes concrete math (50ms × 3.3/sec = 16.5% capacity) and reproduction steps using perf_hooks
Identified additional high-value finding: sequential DB updates with no atomicity (O(n) latency, partial update risk)
Caught the deliveryMap logic bug — possible requirements misunderstanding between single-tracking and batch-tracking, escalated to Bird
Production readiness section is thorough with correct identification of zero monitoring coverage and data_pipeline risk
Idempotency concern properly noted with concrete downstream impact (duplicate notifications, billing events)

agent_output_excerpt:
## Kobe Quality & Risk Review — Carrier Webhook Endpoint

### Summary
**Verdict: BLOCK**
**One-liner:** This endpoint has a critical race condition from early response + unguarded async processing, will block the event loop under load, and silently drops errors — it will fail catastrophically at 200 webhooks/minute.

---

### Critical Findings

#### 1. Fire-and-Forget Async Processing Loses Data Silently
